title: Coca-Cola Meets Sympathetic Judges in Transfer Pricing Dispute
summary: Coca-Cola Meets Sympathetic Judges in Transfer Pricing Dispute news.bloombergtax.com
ai_summary: A US court case involving Coca-Cola's long-standing transfer pricing dispute with the IRS indicates a potentially favorable outcome for the company. The judges appear sympathetic to Coca-Cola's arguments regarding the valuation of intangibles and royalties from its foreign affiliates.

title: Raising the Bar How Transfer Pricing Disputes Are Redefining Tax Governance in Saudi Arabia - Alvarez & Marsal
summary: Raising the Bar How Transfer Pricing Disputes Are Redefining Tax Governance in Saudi Arabia Alvarez & Marsal
ai_summary: Transfer pricing disputes in Saudi Arabia are significantly influencing and reshaping the country's tax governance framework. This trend indicates a growing emphasis on robust TP compliance and documentation for multinational enterprises operating in the region.

title: Vietnam Transfer Pricing Developments: What Multinational Businesses Need to Know in 2026 - Vistra
summary: Vietnam Transfer Pricing Developments: What Multinational Businesses Need to Know in 2026 Vistra
ai_summary: Multinational businesses operating in Vietnam should be aware of ongoing transfer pricing developments and anticipated changes impacting compliance in 2026. These developments highlight Vietnam's evolving regulatory landscape and increased scrutiny on intercompany transactions.

title: Seoul, Hanoi seek to smooth out corporate tax hurdles - The Korea Times
summary: Seoul, Hanoi seek to smooth out corporate tax hurdles The Korea Times
ai_summary: South Korea and Vietnam are engaging in bilateral discussions aimed at resolving corporate tax hurdles faced by businesses operating between the two nations. These efforts are likely to streamline tax compliance and potentially address issues related to double taxation and transfer pricing adjustments.

title: Transfer Pricing: HMRC’s PDCF rebranded and expanded - KPMG
summary: Transfer Pricing: HMRC’s PDCF rebranded and expanded KPMG
ai_summary: HMRC has rebranded and expanded its Profit Diversion Compliance Facility (PDCF), indicating a renewed focus on tackling profit diversion and transfer pricing non-compliance. This development signals a more proactive and potentially broader enforcement approach by the UK tax authority.

title: Mumbai ITAT Reiterates: No Further Profit Attribution to Foreign Enterprise Once Indian AE Is Remunerated at Arm’s Length
summary: The ITAT held that where transactions with the Indian associated enterprise are at arm's length, additional profit attribution to the alleged permanent establishment is unwarranted. The Tribunal therefore deleted the income attributed by the Assessing Officer. The post Mumbai ITAT Reiterates: No Further Profit Attribution to Foreign Enterprise Once Indian AE Is Remunerated at Arm’s Length appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Mumbai ITAT reiterated that no further profit attribution is required for a foreign enterprise's permanent establishment in India if its Indian associated enterprise is already remunerated at arm's length. This ruling provides crucial guidance on the nexus between arm's length remuneration for AEs and profit attribution to PEs in India.

title: ITAT Remands Assessment as AO Rejected Mandatory Section 50C(2) DVO Reference
summary: ITAT Kolkata held that the Assessing Officer was required to refer the property valuation to the DVO when the assessee disputed the value under Section 50C(2). The assessment was remanded for fresh adjudication after complying with the statutory provision. The post ITAT Remands Assessment as AO Rejected Mandatory Section 50C(2) DVO Reference appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Kolkata ITAT remanded an assessment, ruling that the Assessing Officer improperly rejected a mandatory reference to the Departmental Valuation Officer under Section 50C(2) for property valuation. This decision emphasizes adherence to statutory procedures for disputed property valuations in India.

title: TPO Cannot Value Management Fees at Nil Without Rejecting Evidence of Services Rendered
summary: The ITAT Delhi held that the Transfer Pricing Officer could not arbitrarily determine the arm's length price of management services at nil despite evidence establishing receipt of services. The Tribunal restored the assessees TNMM-based benchmarking by following its earlier decision. The post TPO Cannot Value Management Fees at Nil Without Rejecting Evidence of Services Rendered appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Delhi ITAT ruled that a Transfer Pricing Officer cannot arbitrarily value intra-group management fees at nil without properly rejecting evidence of services rendered. The Tribunal reinstated the assessee's TNMM-based benchmarking, reinforcing the need for substantiated transfer pricing adjustments in India.

title: SC Remands Over 1000 Reassessment Appeals After Finance Act 2026 Changed Law
summary: The Supreme Court set aside High Court judgments quashing reassessment notices after noting that the Finance Act, 2026 introduced clarificatory amendments affecting the competence of Assessing Officers. The matters were remanded for fresh consideration, leaving all legal issues open. The post SC Remands Over 1000 Reassessment Appeals After Finance Act 2026 Changed Law appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Indian Supreme Court set aside over 1000 High Court judgments concerning reassessment notices, remanding them for fresh consideration due to clarificatory amendments in the Finance Act 2026. This decision addresses the retrospective application of tax law changes and procedural competence for Assessing Officers.

title: International Controlled Transactions Schedule (ICTS): HMRC launches consultation on 16 June 2026 - Saffery
summary: International Controlled Transactions Schedule (ICTS): HMRC launches consultation on 16 June 2026 Saffery
ai_summary: HMRC has initiated a consultation on the proposed International Controlled Transactions Schedule (ICTS), set to launch on June 16, 2026. This move indicates significant upcoming changes to the reporting and documentation requirements for transfer pricing in the UK.

title: Coca-Cola Meets Sympathetic Judges in Transfer Pricing Dispute
summary: Coca-Cola Meets Sympathetic Judges in Transfer Pricing Dispute news.bloombergtax.com
ai_summary: A US court case involving Coca-Cola's long-standing transfer pricing dispute with the IRS indicates a potentially favorable outcome for the company. The judges appear sympathetic to Coca-Cola's arguments regarding the valuation of intangibles and royalties from its foreign affiliates.

title: Cross-Border ESOPs in India: Unresolved Taxation Gap Silently Costing Mobile Professionals
summary: The article highlights that Indian tax law lacks a statutory formula for allocating cross-border ESOP income, increasing the risk of double taxation. It recommends that the CBDT adopt the OECD workday-based apportionment framework through administrative guidance. The post Cross-Border ESOPs in India: Unresolved Taxation Gap Silently Costing Mobile Professionals appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: This article highlights a gap in Indian tax law regarding the statutory allocation of cross-border ESOP income, which exposes mobile professionals to double taxation risks. It advocates for the Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) to adopt the OECD workday-based apportionment framework through administrative guidance to resolve this issue.

title: ITAT Deletes TP Adjustment as TNMM Was Consistently Accepted in Earlier Years
summary: ITAT Ahmedabad held that the Transactional Net Margin Method (TNMM) remained the most appropriate method as there was no change in the assessee's functional profile and earlier Tribunal decisions had consistently accepted it. The transfer pricing adjustment based on the internal Cost Plus Method was deleted. The post ITAT Deletes TP Adjustment as TNMM Was Consistently Accepted in Earlier Years appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Ahmedabad deleted a transfer pricing adjustment, ruling that the Transactional Net Margin Method (TNMM) remained the most appropriate method for the assessee. The Tribunal emphasized that there was no change in the assessee's functional profile and that earlier decisions had consistently accepted TNMM, overriding an adjustment based on the internal Cost Plus Method.

title: ITAT Deletes Section 69C Addition as Source of Expenditure Was Fully Explained
summary: The ITAT Mumbai held that Section 69C cannot be invoked where expenditure is duly recorded in the books and its source is fully explained. It deleted the addition relating to royalty, commission, and technical service payments. The post ITAT Deletes Section 69C Addition as Source of Expenditure Was Fully Explained appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai held that Section 69C, pertaining to unexplained expenditure, cannot be invoked where expenditure is duly recorded in the books and its source is fully explained. The Tribunal consequently deleted additions related to royalty, commission, and technical service payments, affirming that these expenditures were adequately substantiated.

title: Coca-Cola vs. IRS: $20 Billion Transfer Pricing Tax Battle Heads to Federal Appeals Court - News and Statistics - IndexBox
summary: Coca-Cola vs. IRS: $20 Billion Transfer Pricing Tax Battle Heads to Federal Appeals Court - News and Statistics IndexBox
ai_summary: The long-running $20 billion transfer pricing dispute between Coca-Cola and the IRS is advancing to the Federal Appeals Court. This landmark case centers on the allocation of profits related to valuable marketing intangibles and cost-sharing arrangements.

title: Section 161: Foundation of Transfer Pricing Regime under Income Tax Act, 2025
summary: Learn how Section 161 of the Income-tax Act, 2025 establishes the arm's length principle for international and specified domestic transactions. The provision ensures related-party transactions are taxed at market value to prevent profit shifting. The post Section 161: Foundation of Transfer Pricing Regime under Income Tax Act, 2025 appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: This article explains India's Section 161 of the Income-tax Act, 2025, which establishes the arm's length principle for international and specified domestic transactions. The provision is critical for preventing profit shifting by ensuring related-party transactions are taxed at market value.

title: ITAT Quashes Assessment as Final Order Was Passed Beyond Section 144C(13) Time Limit
summary: Tribunal held that final assessment order was time-barred because it was passed after mandatory period prescribed under Section 144C(13). Assessment was set aside, making remaining transfer pricing issues academic. The post ITAT Quashes Assessment as Final Order Was Passed Beyond Section 144C(13) Time Limit appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Indian ITAT quashed an assessment, including underlying transfer pricing issues, because the final assessment order was passed beyond the mandatory time limit prescribed under Section 144C(13). The procedural lapse rendered the entire assessment time-barred.

title: ITAT Quashes ₹85 Crore TP Adjustment as TPO Order Lacked Digital Signature
summary: The ITAT held that a transfer pricing order issued without authentication or a digital signature is invalid in law. Consequently, it quashed the ₹85 crore transfer pricing adjustment and allowed the assessee's appeals. The post ITAT Quashes ₹85 Crore TP Adjustment as TPO Order Lacked Digital Signature appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Indian ITAT quashed an ₹85 crore transfer pricing adjustment, ruling that a Transfer Pricing Officer (TPO) order issued without a digital signature or proper authentication is invalid in law. This decision highlights the importance of procedural compliance in tax assessments.

title: ITAT v Adopts CBDT-Approved APA Rate for Royalty and FTS ALP
summary: The ITAT held that the royalty and FTS adjustment was excessive and directed the Assessing Officer to apply the 1.9% rate accepted by the CBDT under the Unilateral Advance Pricing Agreement. It treated the APA principles as having persuasive value for determining the arm's length price. The post ITAT v Adopts CBDT-Approved APA Rate for Royalty and FTS ALP appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Indian ITAT directed the Assessing Officer to apply a 1.9% royalty and Fees for Technical Services (FTS) rate, which was previously accepted by the CBDT under a Unilateral Advance Pricing Agreement (APA). The Tribunal considered the APA principles to have persuasive value for determining the arm's length price.

title: Section 263 Revision Invalid as AO Had Already Examined Key Issues: ITAT Visakhapatnam
summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam held that the Commissioner cannot invoke Section 263 where the Assessing Officer has already conducted inquiries and applied his mind to the disputed issues. The post Section 263 Revision Invalid as AO Had Already Examined Key Issues: ITAT Visakhapatnam appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam ruled that a Section 263 revision is invalid if the Assessing Officer had already conducted inquiries and applied due diligence to the disputed issues. This decision limits the Commissioner's power to revise orders where prior examination has occurred.

title: Section 80P Deduction Denied as Valid Return Was Not Filed Under Section 139
summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam held that deduction under Section 80P cannot be allowed where the assessee failed to file a valid return of income, attracting the bar under Section 80A(5). The post Section 80P Deduction Denied as Valid Return Was Not Filed Under Section 139 appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam held that deduction under Section 80P cannot be allowed where the assessee failed to file a valid return of income as required under Section 139. This decision underscores the procedural necessity of proper return filing for claiming tax benefits.

title: Section 80P Deduction Denied as No Valid Return Was Filed: ITAT Visakhapatnam
summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam held that deduction under Section 80P cannot be allowed where the assessee failed to file a valid return of income as required under Section 139 and Section 80A(5). The post Section 80P Deduction Denied as No Valid Return Was Filed: ITAT Visakhapatnam appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam reiterated that a deduction under Section 80P is not permissible if the assessee has failed to file a valid return of income in accordance with Sections 139 and 80A(5). This emphasizes the importance of procedural compliance for claiming tax benefits.

title: Foreign Tax Credit Allowed as Delayed Form 67 Filing Is Procedural: ITAT Visakhapatnam
summary: The Tribunal directed the Assessing Officer to grant Foreign Tax Credit, observing that delayed filing of Form No. 67 is only a procedural requirement and does not extinguish the substantive claim. The post Foreign Tax Credit Allowed as Delayed Form 67 Filing Is Procedural: ITAT Visakhapatnam appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam directed the Assessing Officer to grant Foreign Tax Credit (FTC), ruling that the delayed filing of Form No. 67 is merely a procedural requirement. This decision confirms that a substantive claim for FTC should not be denied solely due to a procedural delay.

title: Pune ITAT Deletes ₹44.80 Crore Angel Tax Addition; AO Cannot Replace Assessee’s DCF Valuation with NAV Method
summary: The Tribunal held that Rule 11UA gives the assessee the exclusive option to choose the valuation method for unquoted shares. While the AO may examine the DCF valuation, he cannot discard it and adopt the NAV method on his own. The post Pune ITAT Deletes ₹44.80 Crore Angel Tax Addition; AO Cannot Replace Assessee’s DCF Valuation with NAV Method appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Pune ITAT deleted a ₹44.80 crore Angel Tax addition, ruling that Rule 11UA grants the assessee the exclusive option to choose the valuation method for unquoted shares. The Tribunal emphasized that the Assessing Officer cannot unilaterally discard the assessee's Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation in favor of the Net Asset Value (NAV) method.

title: ITAT Allows Additional Depreciation on Dumpers as They Form Part of Plant & Machinery
summary: The ITAT Delhi held that dumpers used in manufacturing and mining operations are part of plant and machinery and qualify for additional depreciation under Section 32(1)(iia). It ruled that actual use, and not merely road-going capability, determines eligibility. The post ITAT Allows Additional Depreciation on Dumpers as They Form Part of Plant & Machinery appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi ruled that dumpers used in manufacturing and mining operations qualify as plant and machinery for additional depreciation under Section 32(1)(iia). The tribunal clarified that eligibility is determined by actual use, rather than merely road-going capability.

title: ITAT Directs AO to Follow APA as Royalty Transaction Became Covered Under New Agreement
summary: ITAT held that the Assessing Officer must implement the terms of the later APA covering royalty payments to the AE. The ruling required fresh effect to be given based on the modified return. The post ITAT Directs AO to Follow APA as Royalty Transaction Became Covered Under New Agreement appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT ruled that the Assessing Officer must implement the terms of a later Advance Pricing Agreement (APA) covering royalty payments to an associated enterprise. The tribunal directed that fresh effect be given to the modified tax return in accordance with the APA.

title: ITAT Remands TP Adjustment as AE Transaction ALP Was Computed Using Incorrect Entity-Level Method
summary: The Tribunal held that transfer pricing adjustments must be based on international transactions and not entity-level figures. It remanded the matter after finding errors in the computation of the arm’s length price. The post ITAT Remands TP Adjustment as AE Transaction ALP Was Computed Using Incorrect Entity-Level Method appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT remanded a transfer pricing adjustment, ruling that the arm's length price (ALP) computation must be based on specific international transactions, not entity-level figures. The tribunal identified errors in the original calculation of the arm’s length price, emphasizing a transactional approach.

title: APA Refund Reduces Taxable Royalty, No PE in India: Bombay HC
summary: The Bombay High Court held that only the royalty retained after an APA adjustment is taxable, applying the doctrine of real income. It also ruled that the Indian subsidiary did not constitute a Permanent Establishment under the India-US DTAA. The post APA Refund Reduces Taxable Royalty, No PE in India: Bombay HC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that only the royalty amount retained after an Advance Pricing Agreement (APA) adjustment is subject to tax, applying the doctrine of real income. The court also held that the Indian subsidiary did not constitute a Permanent Establishment (PE) under the India-US Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement.

title: ITAT Delhi Set Aside Transfer Pricing Addition as Selected Comparables Were Functionally Different
summary: ITAT Delhi excluded three comparable companies after holding that they were functionally dissimilar and government-controlled enterprises. Their exclusion eliminated the transfer pricing adjustment on engineering design services. The post ITAT Delhi Set Aside Transfer Pricing Addition as Selected Comparables Were Functionally Different appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi set aside a transfer pricing adjustment by excluding three comparable companies, finding them to be functionally dissimilar and government-controlled enterprises. This exclusion resulted in the elimination of the transfer pricing adjustment related to engineering design services.

summary: CBDT Webinar Decodes New Income-tax Act, 2025 with Global Focus on International Tax & Transfer Pricing Taxscan

This initiative aims to provide clarity on the new legislation's impact on cross-border transactions and compliance.

title: CCDs Are Debt Until Conversion, ITAT allows Interest Deduction Despite RBI FDI Classification
summary: The case examined whether compulsorily convertible debentures should be treated as debt or equity for allowing interest deductions. The Tribunal identified this as the primary issue before determining the arm's length price of interest. The post CCDs Are Debt Until Conversion, ITAT allows Interest Deduction Despite RBI FDI Classification appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Indian Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that Compulsorily Convertible Debentures (CCDs) should be treated as debt until their conversion, allowing interest deductions despite the RBI's FDI classification. The tribunal identified the characterization of CCDs as the primary issue preceding the determination of the arm's length price for interest.

title: Medical Reimbursement Up to ₹15,000 Not Subject to FBT: Madras HC
summary: The Madras High Court ruled that medical reimbursements up to ₹15,000 per employee cannot be subjected to Fringe Benefit Tax in the absence of a clear statutory charging provision. The judgment reiterates that tax cannot be imposed by implication or through administrative circulars. The post Medical Reimbursement Up to ₹15,000 Not Subject to FBT: Madras HC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Madras High Court ruled that medical reimbursements up to ₹15,000 per employee are not subject to Fringe Benefit Tax (FBT) in India. The court emphasized that tax cannot be levied without a clear statutory charging provision.

title: Excess Royalty Refunded Under APA Cannot Be Taxed as Only Retained Amount Is Taxable: Bombay HC
summary: The Bombay High Court held that royalty refunded by a foreign company to its Indian subsidiary under an Advance Pricing Agreement could not be taxed as its income. The Court also ruled that the APA governed the determination of the arm's length price for the covered assessment years. The post Excess Royalty Refunded Under APA Cannot Be Taxed as Only Retained Amount Is Taxable: Bombay HC appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Bombay High Court held that royalty amounts refunded by a foreign company to its Indian subsidiary under an Advance Pricing Agreement (APA) cannot be taxed as income. The court affirmed that only the royalty retained, as determined by the APA, is subject to taxation.

title: ITAT Deletes Transfer Pricing Adjustment as CCDs Are Not Call Options
summary: ITAT Mumbai held that Compulsorily Convertible Debentures cannot be equated with call options and that no option premium arose from their issuance. The transfer pricing adjustment was deleted as no taxable income was generated. The post ITAT Deletes Transfer Pricing Adjustment as CCDs Are Not Call Options appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai deleted a transfer pricing adjustment, ruling that Compulsorily Convertible Debentures (CCDs) cannot be equated with call options. The tribunal concluded that no option premium arose from their issuance, leading to the deletion of the associated transfer pricing adjustment.

title: UAPA Principles must Guide Transfer Pricing Analysis: ITAT restricts Royalty and FTS Adjustment to CBDT-Accepted Rates - Taxscan
summary: UAPA Principles must Guide Transfer Pricing Analysis: ITAT restricts Royalty and FTS Adjustment to CBDT-Accepted Rates Taxscan
ai_summary: The Indian Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that Unilateral Advance Pricing Arrangement (UAPA) principles must guide transfer pricing analysis. This decision specifically restricts royalty and Fees for Technical Services (FTS) adjustments to rates accepted by the CBDT, providing significant guidance for arm's length determinations in India.

title: OECD’s Intragroup Services Rewrite Needs Its Own Benefit Test
summary: OECD’s Intragroup Services Rewrite Needs Its Own Benefit Test news.bloombergtax.com
ai_summary: A practitioner analysis suggests that the OECD's ongoing rewrite of its intragroup services guidance requires a dedicated benefit test to ensure robust arm's length outcomes. This commentary highlights the complexities in applying the benefit test, a fundamental aspect of pricing intercompany service transactions, under revised OECD frameworks.

title: OECD’s new Chapter VII: documentation matters, but transaction understanding comes first - International Tax Review
summary: OECD’s new Chapter VII: documentation matters, but transaction understanding comes first International Tax Review
ai_summary: An International Tax Review article discussing the OECD’s new Chapter VII emphasizes that while robust transfer pricing documentation is essential, a deep understanding of the underlying transactions must be prioritized. The analysis underscores the need for substantive transaction analysis before preparing compliance documentation for intragroup services.

title: ITAT Deletes CCD Interest Disallowance as Debentures Remain Debt Until Conversion
summary: ITAT Mumbai held that Compulsorily Convertible Debentures retain their character as debt until conversion into equity. It deleted the disallowance of interest under Sections 36 and 37 while remanding the transfer pricing benchmarking issue. The post ITAT Deletes CCD Interest Disallowance as Debentures Remain Debt Until Conversion appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai ruled that Compulsorily Convertible Debentures (CCDs) retain their character as debt until conversion into equity, deleting interest disallowance under Sections 36 and 37. While this determined the debt character, the tribunal remanded the transfer pricing benchmarking issue for the interest, indicating further scrutiny of its arm's length nature.

title: Bangalore ITAT Restores Section 50C & Section 54F Issues; CIT(A) Cannot Dismiss Appeal for Non-Prosecution
summary: ITAT Bangalore held that where the assessee disputed the stamp duty valuation and furnished a registered valuer's report, the Assessing Officer ought to have referred the matter to the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO). The decision clarifies procedural aspects for capital gains assessment but does not involve transfer pricing.

title: ITAT Allows Foreign Tax Credit as Delay in Filing Form 67 Is Procedural: ITAT Visakhapatnam
summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam held that delayed filing of Form No. 67 does not justify denial of Foreign Tax Credit where taxes have been paid in the foreign jurisdiction. It ruled that the procedural delay cannot override the substantive benefit available under the DTAA. The post ITAT Allows Foreign Tax Credit as Delay in Filing Form 67 Is Procedural: ITAT Visakhapatnam appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam ruled that a delayed filing of Form No. 67 does not justify denying Foreign Tax Credit (FTC) when taxes have been paid in a foreign jurisdiction. The tribunal held that procedural delays cannot override the substantive benefit available under a Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (DTAA).

title: Section 80P Deduction Denied as Return of Income Was Not Filed Before Assessment
summary: ITAT Visakhapatnam held that filing a return of income before completion of assessment is mandatory for claiming deduction under Section 80P. The Tribunal upheld denial of the deduction where the return was filed only after the assessment. ai_summary: The ITAT Visakhapatnam upheld the denial of a deduction under Section 80P because the return of income was not filed before the completion of assessment. The tribunal emphasized that timely filing of the return is a mandatory condition for claiming specific deductions under Indian tax law.

title: ITAT Remands Capital Gains Case as AO Failed to Refer Valuation to DVO
summary: The ITAT held that the Assessing Officer must comply with the mandatory provisions of Section 50C(2) before adopting the stamp duty value. ai_summary: The ITAT remanded a capital gains case, ruling that the Assessing Officer must comply with the mandatory provisions of Section 50C(2) before adopting the stamp duty value. The matter was sent back for obtaining a valuation report from the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) and fresh adjudication on the property's fair market value.

title: Calcutta HC Orders Form 5 Issuance as Portal Error Cannot Defeat Vivad Se Vishwas Relief
summary: The Calcutta High Court held that the Income Tax Department cannot deny Form 5 under the Vivad Se Vishwas Scheme due to a technical portal error that wrongly mapped the taxpayer's payment. It directed correction of the error and issuance of Form 5 after verification of the challan. The post Calcutta HC Orders Form 5 Issuance as Portal Error Cannot Defeat Vivad Se Vishwas Relief appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Calcutta High Court ordered the Income Tax Department to issue Form 5 under the Vivad Se Vishwas Scheme, stating that a technical portal error should not deny a taxpayer's relief. The court directed the correction of the error and issuance of the form after verifying the challan payment for the tax dispute resolution scheme.

title: Cash Deposits During Demonetisation Cannot Be Taxed Twice Where Sales Are Accepted: ITAT Delhi
summary: The Delhi ITAT held that cash deposits sourced from recorded cash sales cannot be treated as unexplained credits once the sales and books of account are accepted. The Tribunal deleted the Section 68 addition as it resulted in double taxation of the same income. The post Cash Deposits During Demonetisation Cannot Be Taxed Twice Where Sales Are Accepted: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Delhi ITAT ruled that cash deposits sourced from recorded cash sales during demonetization cannot be taxed twice once the sales and books of account are accepted. The tribunal deleted the Section 68 addition, asserting that such an addition would result in double taxation of the same income.

title: Section 50C Applies to Transfer of Land to Govt-Linked Housing Society; ITAT Upholds Section 263 Revision
summary: The ITAT Mumbai held that the doctrine of merger did not bar revision under Section 263 because the applicability of Section 50C was never considered during the reassessment or appellate proceedings. It upheld the revision while directing fresh consideration of the property's valuation through the prescribed statutory mechanism. The post Section 50C Applies to Transfer of Land to Govt-Linked Housing Society; ITAT Upholds Section 263 Revision appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai ruled that Section 50C applies to the transfer of land to a government-linked housing society and upheld a Section 263 revision. The tribunal directed fresh consideration of the property's valuation through the prescribed statutory mechanism, as its applicability was not previously reviewed during assessment or appeal.

title: ITAT Upholds SEB Consumer Tariff for Section 80-IA Deduction on Captive Power Transfers
summary: The ITAT Kolkata held that electricity supplied by captive power plants should be benchmarked using the tariff charged by State Electricity Boards to industrial consumers for computing deduction under Section 80-IA. It upheld deletion of the transfer pricing adjustment by following Supreme Court and Calcutta High Court rulings. The post ITAT Upholds SEB Consumer Tariff for Section 80-IA Deduction on Captive Power Transfers appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Kolkata ruled that the arm's length price for electricity supplied by captive power plants, used for Section 80-IA deduction computations, should be benchmarked against tariffs charged by State Electricity Boards to industrial consumers. This decision upheld the deletion of a transfer pricing adjustment, referencing prior Supreme Court and Calcutta High Court rulings.

title: CSR Donation Eligible for Section 80G Deduction: ITAT Delhi
summary: The ITAT held that foreign exchange gains arising from realization of export proceeds from services rendered to associated enterprises are operating in nature for transfer pricing purposes. It directed verification by the Assessing Officer and corresponding computation of the arm's length margin. The post CSR Donation Eligible for Section 80G Deduction: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi held that foreign exchange gains derived from the realization of export proceeds for services rendered to associated enterprises are operating in nature for transfer pricing purposes. The tribunal directed the Assessing Officer to verify these gains and subsequently compute the arm's length margin.

title: Indian Court Sides With US Gem Co. In Transfer Pricing Row - Law360
summary: Indian Court Sides With US Gem Co. In Transfer Pricing Row Law360
ai_summary: An Indian court has ruled in favor of a US-based gem company in a significant transfer pricing dispute, providing important precedent. The decision sheds light on the application of transfer pricing principles and the arm's length standard within the Indian tax jurisdiction.

title: OECD proposes revisions to intra-group service guidance: Key transfer pricing considerations for MNEs - KPMG
summary: OECD proposes revisions to intra-group service guidance: Key transfer pricing considerations for MNEs KPMG
ai_summary: The OECD has proposed significant revisions to its guidance on intra-group services, impacting key transfer pricing considerations for multinational enterprises. These revisions aim to clarify the application of the arm's length principle to services, potentially influencing how MNEs structure and price their internal service arrangements globally.

title: Transfer Pricing Addition on Management Fees Unsustainable When Expenditure Capitalized and Not Claimed as Deduction: ITAT Deletes ₹1.04 Crore Adjustment - Taxscan
summary: Transfer Pricing Addition on Management Fees Unsustainable When Expenditure Capitalized and Not Claimed as Deduction: ITAT Deletes ₹1.04 Crore Adjustment Taxscan
ai_summary: The Indian Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) deleted a transfer pricing adjustment of ₹1.04 Crore related to management fees. The tribunal ruled that the addition was unsustainable as the expenditure was capitalized and not claimed as a deduction by the taxpayer, emphasizing the linkage between accounting treatment and transfer pricing deductibility.

title: ITAT Deletes ₹76.45 Crore TP Addition as CCDs Cannot Be Recharacterized as Equity
summary: ITAT Mumbai held that Compulsorily Convertible Debentures could not be treated as equity merely to deny interest deductions. The Tribunal deleted the ₹76.45 crore transfer pricing adjustment arising from such recharacterization. The post ITAT Deletes ₹76.45 Crore TP Addition as CCDs Cannot Be Recharacterized as Equity appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Mumbai deleted a ₹76.45 crore transfer pricing adjustment, ruling that Compulsorily Convertible Debentures (CCDs) cannot be recharacterized as equity simply to deny interest deductions. This decision emphasizes the importance of respecting the legal form of financial instruments for transfer pricing purposes.

title: ITAT Partly Allows TP Appeal, Revises Comparable Selection for ALP Determination
summary: The ITAT found inconsistencies in the selection and rejection of comparable companies for determining the arm’s length price of international transactions. It directed inclusion and exclusion of specific comparables and partly allowed the assessee’s appeal. The post ITAT Partly Allows TP Appeal, Revises Comparable Selection for ALP Determination appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT partly allowed a transfer pricing appeal, finding inconsistencies in the selection and rejection of comparable companies for determining the arm’s length price of international transactions. The Tribunal provided specific directions for the inclusion and exclusion of comparables, refining the benchmarking analysis.

title: Section 50C Addition Upheld as Seized Cash Confirmed On-Money Receipt
summary: ITAT sustained the adoption of fair market value under Section 50C after finding that seized cash represented on-money from property transactions. The Tribunal upheld the valuation determined through the DVO process. The post Section 50C Addition Upheld as Seized Cash Confirmed On-Money Receipt appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT upheld an addition under Section 50C after confirming that seized cash represented undeclared 'on-money' from property transactions. The Tribunal sustained the adoption of the fair market value determined through the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) process.
